Parameter: Mention 5 Major Diseases and Severity (Low, Medium, High)
Arecanut: Fruit Rot / Mahali (High), Yellow Leaf Disease (High), Bud Rot (Medium), Ganoderma / Foot Rot (Medium), Stem Bleeding (Low)
Cocoa: Black Pod (High), Stem Canker (Medium), Vascular Streak Dieback (High), Witches Broom (Medium), Root Rot (Low)
Coffee: Coffee Leaf Rust (High), Black Rot (Medium), Root Rot (Medium), Cercospora Leaf Spot (Low), Dieback (Medium)
Tea: Blister Blight (High), Black Rot (Medium), Grey Blight (Medium), Red Rust (Low), Root Rot (High)
Pepper: Foot Rot / Quick Wilt (High), Slow Wilt (High), Pollu Disease (Medium), Stunt Disease (Medium), Phyllody (Low)
Cloves: Dieback (High), Leaf Spot (Medium), Leaf Rot (Medium), Seedling Wilt (Low), Root Rot (Low)
Betel Leaf: Foot Rot / Wilt (High), Leaf Spot (High), Anthracnose (Medium), Bacterial Leaf Spot (High), Stem Rot (Medium)
Cardamom: Katte / Mosaic Virus (High), Capsule Rot / Azhukal (High), Rhizome Rot (High), Chenthal (Medium), Damping Off (Medium)

Parameter: Nutrient (Mineral) Deficiencies and Their Associated Syndrome
Arecanut: Mg: Interveinal chlorosis of older leaves; Zn: Rosetting/small leaves; N: Pale green foliage
Cocoa: Zn: Small/sickle shaped leaves; N: Yellowing of leaves; K: Marginal necrosis
Coffee: Zn: Rosetting/small leaves; Mg: Interveinal chlorosis; B: Tip death/poor fruit set
Tea: N: Yellowing; K: Marginal scorching; Zn: Sickle shaped leaves; Mg: Chlorosis
Pepper: N: Yellowing of older leaves; Mg: Interveinal chlorosis; K: Tip burn
Betel Leaf: N: Stunted growth, pale leaves; K: Leaf margin scorching; Mg: Interveinal yellowing
Cardamom: N: Yellowing; K: Leaf tip/edge necrosis; Zn: Reduced leaf size/rosetting; B: Poor capsule setting
